input.prompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate

input.initialPrompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

data.content:
Here's a complete example:

**XAML**
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.Views.ItemsPage">

  <CollectionView ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"
                  SelectionMode="Single"
                  SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem, Mode=TwoWay}"
                  IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}"
                  RemainingItemsThreshold="0">

    <!-- Pull-to-refresh via a wrapper RefreshView -->
    <CollectionView.Parent>
      <!-- Actually, wrap the CollectionView: -->
    </CollectionView.Parent>
  </CollectionView>
</ContentPage>
```

The correct pattern is to **wrap** `CollectionView` in a `RefreshView`:

```xml
<RefreshView Command="{Binding RefreshCommand}"
             IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}">

  <CollectionView ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"
                  SelectionMode="Single"
                  SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem, Mode=TwoWay}">
    <CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
      <DataTemplate>
        <Grid Padding="12" ColumnDefinitions="*,Auto">
          <Label Text="{Binding Name}" FontSize="16" />
          <Label Grid.Column="1" Text="{Binding Status}"
                 TextColor="Gray" />
        </Grid>
      </DataTemplate>
    </CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
  </CollectionView>

</RefreshView>
```

**ViewModel**
```csharp
public partial class ItemsViewModel : ObservableObject
{
    private readonly IApiService _api;
    private readonly INavigationService _nav;

    [ObservableProperty]
    private ObservableCollection<Item> _items = new();

    [ObservableProperty]
    private bool _isRefreshing;

    [ObservableProperty]
    private Item? _selectedItem;

    // React to selection changes → navigate
    partial void OnSelectedItemChanged(Item? value)
    {
        if (value is null) return;

        // Navigate, then clear selection so the user can re-select
        _nav.NavigateAsync(nameof(DetailPage), 
            new Dictionary<string, object> { ["Item"] = value });
        SelectedItem = null;
    }

    // Pull-to-refresh command
    [RelayCommand]
    private async Task RefreshAsync()
    {
        try
        {
            var fresh = await _api.GetItemsAsync();
            Items = new ObservableCollection<Item>(fresh);
        }
        finally
        {
            IsRefreshing = false;   // stops the spinner
        }
    }
}
```

**Key bindings explained:**

| Binding | Direction | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `ItemsSource → Items` | OneWay | Displays the collection |
| `SelectedItem → SelectedItem` | **TwoWay** | Captures selection *and* lets you clear it (`= null`) after navigating |
| `IsRefreshing → IsRefreshing` | TwoWay | RefreshView sets it `true` on pull; you set it `false` when done |
| `Command → RefreshCommand` | OneWay | Triggers your async reload |

**Important details:**

1. **Set `IsRefreshing = false`** in a `finally` block — otherwise the spinner hangs on error.
2. **Clear `SelectedItem` after navigating** — without this, tapping the same item twice won't fire a second selection change.
3. Uses **CommunityToolkit.Mvvm** (`[ObservableProperty]`, `[RelayCommand]`) to eliminate boilerplate. Without the toolkit, implement `INotifyPropertyChanged` and `ICommand` manually.
